steps: Long exact sequence: $\cdots \to H_k(S^{n-1}) \to H_k(D^n) \to H_k(D^n, S^{n-1}) \xrightarrow{\partial} H_{k-1}(S^{n-1}) \to \cdots$
For $k \geq 2$: $0 \to H_k(D^n, S^{n-1}) \xrightarrow{\cong} H_{k-1}(S^{n-1}) \to 0$. So $H_k(D^n, S^{n-1}) \cong H_{k-1}(S^{n-1})$.
$H_{k-1}(S^{n-1}) = \mathbb{Z}$ for $k-1 \in \{0, n-1\}$, i.e.\ $k = 1$ (but $H_1(D^n, S^{n-1})$ needs care) or $k = n$.
At $k = n$: $H_n(D^n, S^{n-1}) \cong H_{n-1}(S^{n-1}) = \mathbb{Z}$. All other groups vanish.
Alternatively: by excision, $H_*(D^n, S^{n-1}) \cong \tilde{H}_*(D^n/S^{n-1}) = \tilde{H}_*(S^n)$, giving $\mathbb{Z}$ in degree $n$.
